Shallow Copy: Shallow Copy stores the references of objects to the original memory address.
Deep Copy: Deep copy stores copies of the object’s value.

Shallow Copy: Shallow Copy reflects changes made to the new/copied object in the original object.
Deep Copy: Deep copy doesn’t reflect changes made to the new/copied object in the original object.

Shallow Copy: Shallow Copy stores the copy of the original object and points the references to the objects.
Deep Copy: Deep copy stores the copy of the original object and recursively copies the objects as well.
